binaries.title: dxgmms2.sys - Use-After-Free in VidSch sync-point command tracking (linked-list node freed without unlinking on allocation failure)

changed_functions.name: VidSchiAddPendingCommandToSyncPointList (sub_1C0014848)
key_changes: REORDERED OPERATIONS (feature-gated): Unpatched inserts the newly allocated 0xa8-byte tracking node (rbx) into a doubly-linked list at arg1+0x648 BEFORE allocating its two sub-arrays (+0x18 allocation records, arg4 entries x 0x10; +0x48 range records, arg5 entries x 0x18). Patched, when the feature is enabled, allocates both sub-arrays first and inserts the node into the list only after both succeed (deferred insertion at 0x1c000fb20).

NEW GATE FUNCTION: Patched calls Feature_1859884346__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage (sub_1c0018f04), a WIL feature-staging IsEnabled check backed by the static variable Feature_1859884346__private_featureState (with Feature_1859884346__private_IsEnabledFallback). It controls whether the safe deferred-insertion path or the original insert-before-allocate path is taken.

ERROR PATH: When either sub-array allocation fails, the node is freed via the _VIDSCH_SYNC_POINT scalar deleting destructor (sub_1c0029df0 unpatched / sub_1c0029e60 patched), which frees the node and its sub-arrays with ExFreePoolWithTag but performs no RemoveEntryList. In the unpatched build the node is still linked at that point, leaving a dangling pointer; in the patched enabled path the node is not yet linked, so freeing is safe.
Register reallocation: r15d/r14d/r13d usage shifted to accommodate the restructured control flow (non-behavioral).
changed_functions.security_notes: USE-AFTER-FREE (CWE-416): VidSchiAddPendingCommandToSyncPointList (sub_1C0014848) records the allocations referenced by a video-scheduler device command into a per-device sync-point list. It allocates a 0xa8-byte node (ExAllocatePoolWithTag, tag 'Viaf' = 0x66616956, NonPagedPoolNx), sets the key at +0x10 and initializes embedded sub-lists at +0x88/+0x98, then inserts the node into a doubly-linked list whose head LIST_ENTRY is at arg1+0x648 (Flink) / arg1+0x650 (Blink, the tail pointer) BEFORE allocating two variable-length sub-arrays: +0x18 (allocation records, arg4 x 0x10 bytes) and +0x48 (range records, arg5 x 0x18 bytes), both tag 'Via5' = 0x35616956. If either ExAllocatePoolWithTag returns NULL, the error path calls the _VIDSCH_SYNC_POINT scalar deleting destructor sub_1c0029df0(rbx), which frees the node with ExFreePoolWithTag but does NOT unlink it (no RemoveEntryList). The freed node stays chained: the previous node's Flink and arg1->list tail (arg1+0x650) still reference it. The next device-command submission reads arg1+0x650 and compares [rbx+0x10] against the new key, dereferencing freed pool memory. The patched build fixes this under a WIL feature gate: when Feature_1859884346__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage returns enabled, the node is linked only after both sub-arrays are allocated, so the failure path frees an unlinked node.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
=== UNPATCHED: node linked into list BEFORE sub-array allocation ===

; Node allocation
0x1c0014922: mov edx, 0A8h                   ; NumberOfBytes
0x1c0014927: mov ecx, r12d                   ; NonPagedPoolNx (0x200)
0x1c001492a: mov r8d, 66616956h              ; tag 'Viaf'
0x1c0014930: call ExAllocatePoolWithTag

; Node init + UNCONDITIONAL list insertion (no allocation guard)
0x1c0014966: mov [rbx+10h], rsi              ; rbx->key = arg3
0x1c0014986: mov rax, [rdi+8]                ; rax = tail (head->Blink), rdi = arg1+0x648
0x1c001498a: cmp [rax], rdi                  ; verify tail->Flink == head
0x1c001498d: jnz 0x1c002142b                 ; FailFast
0x1c0014993: mov [rbx], rdi                  ; rbx->Flink = head
0x1c001499f: mov [rax], rbx                  ; old_tail->Flink = rbx  *** NODE NOW IN LIST ***
0x1c00149a2: mov [rdi+8], rbx                ; head->Blink = rbx (tail = rbx)

; THEN sub-array allocations (cold block)
0x1c00211b0: shl rdx, 4                       ; arg4 * 16
0x1c00211b7: call ExAllocatePoolWithTag       ; tag 'Via5' -> +0x18 (may FAIL)
0x1c00211ef: call ExAllocatePoolWithTag       ; tag 'Via5', arg5*0x18 -> +0x48 (may FAIL)

; ERROR PATH: free without unlinking
0x1c0021411: call ExFreePoolWithTag           ; free +0x18 sub-array (rcx = r12)
0x1c0021175: call ExFreePoolWithTag           ; free +0x48 sub-array (rcx = rsi; only if arg5 > 2)
0x1c0021181: mov rcx, rbx                     ; rbx = node STILL IN THE LIST
0x1c0021184: call 0x1c0029df0                 ; _VIDSCH_SYNC_POINT dtor: frees rbx, NO unlink
0x1c0021189: mov eax, 0C0000017h              ; STATUS_INSUFFICIENT_RESOURCES

=== PATCHED: feature-gated deferred insertion ===

0x1c000f99d: call ExAllocatePoolWithTag        ; 0xA8, tag 'Viaf'
0x1c000f9d2: mov [rbx+10h], rsi                ; key = arg3

; Gate #1: skip early insertion when feature enabled
0x1c000f9f2: call Feature_1859884346__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage
0x1c000f9f7: test eax, eax
0x1c000f9f9: jnz 0x1c000fa16                    ; enabled -> jump to allocation, do NOT link
0x1c000fa08: mov [rbx], rdi                     ; (disabled path) link before alloc
0x1c000fa0f: mov [rax], rbx

; Allocate sub-arrays
0x1c000fa43: call ExAllocatePoolWithTag         ; +0x18, tag 'Via5', arg4<<4
0x1c000fab1: call ExAllocatePoolWithTag         ; +0x48, tag 'Via5', arg5*0x18

; Check both succeeded
0x1c000faf8: test r12, r12                      ; if (!P_alloc)
0x1c000fafb: jz 0x1c000fe67
0x1c000fb01: test r14, r14                      ; if (!P_range)
0x1c000fb04: jz 0x1c000fe67

; Gate #2: insert only after allocs succeed
0x1c000fb0a: call Feature_1859884346__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage
0x1c000fb11: jz 0x1c000fb2e                      ; disabled -> already linked, skip
0x1c000fb20: mov [rbx], rdi                      ; rbx->Flink = head
0x1c000fb27: mov [rax], rbx                      ; *** LINK INTO LIST (safe) ***
0x1c000fb2a: mov [rdi+8], rbx                    ; tail = rbx

; Cleanup on allocation failure
0x1c000fe67: call Feature_1859884346__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage
0x1c000fe9f: call sub_1c0029e60                  ; _VIDSCH_SYNC_POINT dtor (node unlinked when enabled)

security_findings.entry_point: Video scheduler device-command submission. VidMM operations that offer or terminate allocations (VIDMM_GLOBAL::OfferOneAllocation at 0x1c0063df0, VIDMM_GLOBAL::TerminateAllocation at 0x1c0062a60, VIDMM_GLOBAL::TerminateOneAllocation at 0x1c0088064) call VidSchSubmitDeviceCommand (0x1c000ed90) with a direct call, which calls VidSchiAddPendingCommandToSyncPointList to record the command's referenced allocations into a per-device sync-point list. These VidMM functions sit under the module entry points for D3DKMT offer/destroy/terminate, so the path is reachable from a standard-user process.
call_chain: User-mode D3DKMT API (e.g. D3DKMTOfferAllocations / allocation-destruction) -> dxgkrnl.sys -> dxgmms2.sys
VidSchSubmitDeviceCommand (sub_1c000ed90) - builds the scheduler device command
VidSchiAddPendingCommandToSyncPointList (sub_1c0014848) - VULNERABLE: allocates tracking node, inserts into per-device list, allocates sub-arrays, frees without unlinking on failure
security_findings.vulnerability_class: Use-After-Free (CWE-416) - linked-list node freed without removal, leaving dangling pointers
security_findings.description: VidSchiAddPendingCommandToSyncPointList (0x1C0014848) records the allocations referenced by a video-scheduler device command into a per-device sync-point list. The function: (1) Allocates a 0xa8-byte node (ExAllocatePoolWithTag, tag 'Viaf' = 0x66616956, NonPagedPoolNx). (2) Sets the key at +0x10 = arg3 and initializes embedded doubly-linked sub-lists at +0x88 and +0x98. (3) Inserts the node into a global per-device doubly-linked list whose head LIST_ENTRY is at arg1+0x648 (Flink) and arg1+0x650 (Blink, i.e. the tail pointer) - this insertion is unconditional and happens before the node's sub-array is allocated. (4) THEN fills two variable-length sub-arrays: +0x18 (allocation records, arg4 entries x 0x10 bytes) and +0x48 (range records, arg5 entries x 0x18 bytes), both tag 'Via5' = 0x35616956; each sub-array is heap-allocated only when its count exceeds 2, otherwise embedded storage at +0x20 / +0x50 is used (no allocation, cannot fail). On the reachable call paths arg5 is a constant 0, so only the +0x18 sub-array (size arg4 << 4) is heap-allocated, and only when arg4 > 2. (5) If that ExAllocatePoolWithTag returns NULL, execution reaches the error cleanup near 0x1c002140c, which frees the sub-array(s) if non-NULL, then calls the _VIDSCH_SYNC_POINT scalar deleting destructor at 0x1c0029df0(rbx) to free the node. That destructor only calls ExFreePoolWithTag on the node and its sub-arrays; it performs no RemoveEntryList to unlink the node from arg1+0x648. The freed node's Flink/Blink still chain it into the live list, and arg1->list tail (arg1+0x650) still points at the freed node. The next traversal of this list - including the same function called for a subsequent device-command submission, which reads rbx = *(arg1 + 0x650) at entry and either compares [rbx+0x10] against the new key (0x1c00148a7) or reads the freed tail's Flink on the re-link path (0x1c0014986) - dereferences freed pool memory, constituting a use-after-free. The patched build defers the list insertion until after the sub-array is allocated when the Feature_1859884346 device-usage flag is enabled; when the flag is disabled the original insert-before-allocate ordering is retained.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: 1. Open a DirectX graphics adapter device handle from user mode (D3DKMTOpenAdapter). 2. Create video-memory allocations referenced by the graphics scheduler. 3. Perform an operation that submits a scheduler device command - e.g. D3DKMTOfferAllocations or allocation destruction, which reach VIDMM_GLOBAL::OfferOneAllocation / TerminateOneAllocation / TerminateAllocation -> VidSchSubmitDeviceCommand -> VidSchiAddPendingCommandToSyncPointList. VidSchSubmitDeviceCommand derives arg4 (the +0x18 allocation-record count) by walking the device's tracked-entry list at arg1+0x48, and passes arg5 = 0. 4. Cause enough qualifying entries that arg4 > 2, so the +0x18 sub-array (arg4 << 4 bytes, tag 'Via5') is a heap allocation, and create NonPagedPoolNx memory pressure so it returns NULL. This triggers the error path that frees the node while it is still linked at arg1+0x648. 5. Submit another device command - the traversal of the corrupted list dereferences the freed node. The demonstrable outcome is a kernel bug check (denial of service). The trigger depends on forcing a NonPagedPoolNx allocation failure and is non-deterministic.
security_findings.primitive: Use-After-Free of a 0xa8-byte NonPagedPoolNx chunk (tag 'Viaf'). After the error path frees the node, arg1+0x650 (list tail) and the previous node's Flink still reference the freed chunk; the next list traversal dereferences freed pool memory. The reliably reachable outcome is a kernel crash (denial of service) from a standard-user process. Escalation to memory corruption would require reclaiming the freed 0xa8-byte chunk with controlled contents in the window between the free and the next traversal (at which point +0x10, the +0x18/+0x48 sub-array pointers, and the +0x88/+0x98 sub-list heads would be read as node state); that reclaim, under the required memory-pressure trigger, and any resulting read/write primitive are not demonstrated here.
security_findings.assembly:
VidSchiAddPendingCommandToSyncPointList (unpatched, sub_1c0014848) key sections:

; === NODE ALLOCATION ===
0x1c0014922: mov edx, 0A8h                   ; NumberOfBytes
0x1c001492a: mov r8d, 66616956h              ; tag 'Viaf'
0x1c0014930: call ExAllocatePoolWithTag

; === LINKED LIST INSERTION (BEFORE allocation - THE BUG) ===
0x1c0014966: mov [rbx+10h], rsi              ; rbx->key = arg3
0x1c0014986: mov rax, [rdi+8]                ; rax = tail (head->Blink), rdi = arg1+0x648
0x1c001498a: cmp [rax], rdi                  ; verify tail->Flink == head
0x1c001498d: jnz 0x1c002142b                 ; FailFast
0x1c0014993: mov [rbx], rdi                  ; rbx->Flink = head
0x1c001499f: mov [rax], rbx                  ; old_tail->Flink = rbx  *** IN LIST ***
0x1c00149a2: mov [rdi+8], rbx                ; head->Blink = rbx (tail = rbx)

; === SUB-ARRAY ALLOCATION (AFTER insertion, cold block) ===
0x1c00211b0: shl rdx, 4                       ; arg4 * 16
0x1c00211b7: call ExAllocatePoolWithTag       ; tag 'Via5' -> +0x18
0x1c00211ef: call ExAllocatePoolWithTag       ; tag 'Via5', arg5*0x18 -> +0x48

; === ERROR PATH: free without unlink ===
0x1c0021411: call ExFreePoolWithTag           ; free +0x18 sub-array (rcx = r12)
0x1c0021175: call ExFreePoolWithTag           ; free +0x48 sub-array (rcx = rsi; only if arg5 > 2)
0x1c0021181: mov rcx, rbx                     ; node STILL IN LIST
0x1c0021184: call 0x1c0029df0                 ; _VIDSCH_SYNC_POINT dtor: frees rbx, NO unlink *** UAF ***
0x1c0021189: mov eax, 0C0000017h              ; STATUS_INSUFFICIENT_RESOURCES

; === 0x1c0029df0 (_VIDSCH_SYNC_POINT scalar deleting destructor) ===
0x1c0029df9: mov rcx, [rcx+48h]              ; +0x48 sub-array
0x1c0029e0d: call ExFreePoolWithTag           ; free sub-array (if not embedded)
0x1c0029e29: mov rcx, [rbx+18h]              ; +0x18 sub-array
0x1c0029e39: call ExFreePoolWithTag           ; free sub-array (if not embedded)
0x1c0029e58: call ExFreePoolWithTag(rbx, 0)   ; free NODE - no RemoveEntryList anywhere

breakpoints.why: Function entry (sub_1c0014848). rcx=arg1 (_VIDSCH_DEVICE; list head at +0x648, tail pointer at +0x650), rdx=arg2 (VIDMM_ALLOC*), r8=arg3 (key/fence), r9d=arg4 (allocation count -> +0x18 sub-array size = r9d << 4), arg5 (range count -> +0x48 sub-array size = arg5 * 0x18), arg6 (command type, selects +0x88 vs +0x98 sub-list). Watch arg4/arg5 to gauge sub-array sizes.

'mov [rbx], rdi' links the node into the per-device list BEFORE the sub-arrays are allocated. rbx = new node, rdi = arg1+0x648 (list head), rax = old tail. Once this executes the node is orphaned if a later allocation fails.
'call sub_1c0029df0' frees the node without unlinking. When this hits, rbx still has Flink/Blink pointing into the live list; after the call arg1+0x650 is a dangling pointer.

The cleanup routine (sub_1c0029df0). rcx = node to free. Confirm its Flink (+0x00) and Blink (+0x08) still point at live list entries, proving no unlink. Step to the final ExFreePoolWithTag to see the node freed while still linked.

'mov rbx, [rcx+0x650]' reads the list tail pointer at entry. If a prior call left a freed node linked, this loads a pointer to freed memory; the subsequent 'cmp [rbx+0x10], rsi' at 0x1c00148a7 dereferences the freed node - the UAF read.

watch: rcx at function entry = arg1 (_VIDSCH_DEVICE; list head at +0x648, tail pointer at +0x650)
r9d = arg4 = allocation count (determines +0x18 sub-array size: r9d << 4 bytes)
arg5 = range count (determines +0x48 sub-array size: arg5 * 0x18 bytes)
rbx after 0x1c0014993 = the newly linked node (will be freed if allocs fail)
rbx at 0x1c0021184 = the node about to be freed while still in the list (UAF trigger)
rax at ExAllocatePoolWithTag return = NULL indicates the sub-array allocation failure that reaches the vulnerable error path
[arg1+0x650] = list tail pointer - after the UAF this points to freed memory
key_offsets: 0x1c0014993: 'mov [rbx], rdi' - node linked into list BEFORE allocation (root cause)
0x1c001499f: 'mov [rax], rbx' - old_tail->Flink = rbx, completing the link
0x1c0021411 / 0x1c0021175: 'call ExFreePoolWithTag' - free the +0x18 / +0x48 sub-arrays in the error path
0x1c0021184: 'call sub_1c0029df0' - free node WITHOUT unlinking (THE BUG)
0x1c0014894: 'mov rbx, [rcx+0x650]' - list tail read that loads the freed node on a later call
0x1c00148a7: 'cmp [rbx+0x10], rsi' - UAF read of the freed node's key field at +0x10
debugging_aids.trigger_setup: 1. From a user-mode process, create a D3D device and a large number of video-memory allocations referenced by the scheduler. 2. Perform an operation that submits a scheduler device command referencing them - e.g. D3DKMTOfferAllocations or allocation/resource destruction, reaching VIDMM_GLOBAL::OfferOneAllocation / TerminateAllocation -> VidSchSubmitDeviceCommand -> VidSchiAddPendingCommandToSyncPointList. The counts arg4 (allocations) and arg5 (ranges) scale with how many allocations the command references. 3. Force allocation failure: exhaust NonPagedPoolNx (spray from multiple processes) or use a large enough count that arg4 << 4 or arg5 * 0x18 is a large pool request likely to fail. 4. The error path frees the node while it remains linked at arg1+0x648. 5. Submit another device command - the function re-reads arg1+0x650 (tail) and dereferences the freed node at +0x10, triggering the UAF. 6. For exploitation, spray the 0xa8-byte NonPagedPoolNx allocation with controlled data (named-pipe buffers, NtCreateWorkerFactory objects, etc.) between the free and the re-traversal to control the dereferenced fields.
debugging_aids.expected_observation: Kernel bug check PAGE_FAULT_IN_NONPAGED_AREA (0x50) or POOL_CORRUPTION (0x19) when the freed node is dereferenced on the next list traversal; the faulting address falls within the freed node allocation. '!pool <addr>' shows the allocation as freed; track the node with '!poolused 2 Viaf' / '!poolfind Viaf'. The demonstrable outcome is denial of service; any reclaim of the freed chunk with controlled data before the traversal is not demonstrated here.
debugging_aids.notes: The node pool tag is 'Viaf' (0x66616956) and the sub-array tag is 'Via5' (0x35616956). The patched guard Feature_1859884346__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage (sub_1c0018f04) is a WIL feature-staging IsEnabled check that reads the static variable Feature_1859884346__private_featureState (with a Feature_1859884346__private_IsEnabledFallback path); it is not a WNF query. This gate is what selects the corrective ordering: when the feature is enabled the node is linked only after both sub-arrays succeed; when disabled the insert-before-allocate ordering is retained. The caller VidSchSubmitDeviceCommand (sub_1c000ed90) submits a scheduler device command, so the failure is a deterministic logic path (missing unlink before free), not a race.

summary: The patch addresses a Use-After-Free (CWE-416, Medium) in dxgmms2.sys in the video-scheduler sync-point command-tracking path. VidSchiAddPendingCommandToSyncPointList (0x1C0014848 unpatched / 0x1C000F918 patched) allocates a 0xa8-byte tracking node (tag 'Viaf') and inserts it into a per-device doubly-linked list (head at arg1+0x648, tail pointer at arg1+0x650) BEFORE allocating its variable-length sub-array. On the reachable call paths arg5 is 0, so only the +0x18 sub-array (arg4 records, tag 'Via5', size arg4<<4) is heap-allocated, and only when arg4>2 (arg4 is a workload-scaled count computed in VidSchSubmitDeviceCommand). If that allocation fails under memory pressure, the error path frees the node via the _VIDSCH_SYNC_POINT scalar deleting destructor (0x1c0029df0) WITHOUT unlinking it, leaving a dangling pointer at arg1+0x650; the next device-command submission traverses the list and dereferences freed memory. The patched build adds a WIL feature-staging gate, Feature_1859884346__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage (0x1C0018F04): when enabled, the sub-array is allocated first and the node is linked only after it succeeds; when disabled the original insert-before-allocate ordering is retained (staged rollout). Reachability is confirmed: the function is reached only from VidSchSubmitDeviceCommand (0x1C000ED90), called directly by VIDMM_GLOBAL::OfferOneAllocation/TerminateAllocation/TerminateOneAllocation under the VidMm module entry points for D3DKMT offer/destroy/terminate. Demonstrable impact is a kernel crash (denial of service); escalation beyond DoS requires reclaiming the freed chunk within the pre-traversal window and is not demonstrated.
